title: Light and Dark Sensor
subtitle: Observe how light changes electricity!
overview: Students explore how a photoresistor (LDR) reacts to light and darkness. By covering the sensor with a finger, students observe the LED becoming brighter and understand how light sensors are used in real life.

images._note: Add actual classroom activity photos, LDR circuits, LEDs glowing, and students testing the sensor.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: LED glowing brightly in dark condition
gallery.caption: LED becomes brighter in darkness

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student covering LDR with finger
gallery.caption: Students testing light and dark sensor behavior
Automatic street lights use light sensors
Phones use light sensors for screen brightness
Night lamps turn ON automatically in darkness

keyTopics: What is a Light Sensor (LDR / Photoresistor)
LDR changes resistance based on light
Automatic street lights use LDR sensors
Mobile phones adjust brightness using light sensors

activity.title: Cover the Sensor — Watch the LED Glow!
steps: Connect the LDR on the breadboard
Observe LED brightness in normal light
Watch LED glow brighter in darkness
Discuss why the LED changes brightness

quiz.explanation: LDR means Light Dependent Resistor — its resistance changes with light.

quiz.q: What happens when we cover the photoresistor?
quiz.explanation: In darkness, the LED becomes brighter because the LDR resistance changes.
LDR sensors react to light intensity.

quiz.q: Where are light sensors used in real life?
quiz.explanation: Light sensors are used in many smart devices.

quiz.q: In bright light, LDR resistance becomes:
quiz.explanation: More light means lower resistance in an LDR.
Darkness increases the resistance of the photoresistor.

quiz.q: Why do phones use light sensors?
quiz.explanation: Phones use light sensors to control brightness automatically.

quiz.q: What is the main job of a sensor?
quiz.explanation: Sensors detect environmental changes like light, sound, or temperature.

quiz.q: Which component glows in this activity?
quiz.explanation: The LED changes brightness during the experiment.

quiz.q: Automatic street lights turn ON during:
quiz.explanation: Street lights use LDR sensors to detect darkness.

quiz.q: True or False: LDR sensors can detect sound.
quiz.explanation: LDR sensors detect light, not sound.

quiz.q: What covers the sensor in the activity?
quiz.explanation: Students use their finger to block light from reaching the sensor.

options: The sensor reacts to light changes
quiz.explanation: The LDR changes resistance based on light levels.

quiz.q: Which of these is a smart use of light sensors?
quiz.explanation: Light sensors are widely used in smart systems.

quiz.q: What did students observe in this experiment?
quiz.explanation: Students learned that light changes how the circuit behaves.
